description:
You are a database administrator who has just spent the afternoon refactoring slow-running SQL statements.  
  Before deploying the new query plan to production, you must validate that the Certificate Authority (CA) file
  used by psql for encrypted connections has not been altered during testing.

  Your mission
  1. Create a directory named “db_ssl” directly under your home directory: /home/user/db_ssl  
  2. Copy the CA file that QA provided, located at /home/user/sample_certs/ca.pem, into the new directory.  
  3. Calculate the SHA-256 checksum of the copied certificate and write the result to
     /home/user/db_ssl/ca_checksum.log using the *exact* two-line format shown below.

  Exact log-file format (including capitalisation and the newline placement)
  ----------------------------------------------------
  CA_FILE=/home/user/db_ssl/ca.pem
  SHA256=<64-character-hex-digest>
  ----------------------------------------------------
  • CA_FILE must be the absolute path shown above.  
  • SHA256 must be precisely the 64-character hexadecimal digest produced by sha256sum
    when run on /home/user/db_ssl/ca.pem (no extra spaces, tabs, or trailing characters).  
  • No extra lines or commentary are allowed in the log file.

  The automated grader will:
  • Confirm that /home/user/db_ssl exists.  
  • Confirm that /home/user/db_ssl/ca.pem exists and is a byte-for-byte copy of /home/user/sample_certs/ca.pem.  
  • Recompute the SHA-256 digest of /home/user/db_ssl/ca.pem and compare it with the value in
    /home/user/db_ssl/ca_checksum.log.  
  • Verify that the log file contains exactly two lines in the format above.

  Hint: fewer than three shell commands are sufficient to accomplish the entire task.
truth:
Initial files present before the task starts
  -------------------------------------------
  1. Directory: /home/user/sample_certs
  2. File: /home/user/sample_certs/ca.pem

     Exact byte contents (5 bytes total, no trailing newline)
     --------------------------------------------------------
     test
     --------------------------------------------------------

  Ground-truth SHA-256 digest of /home/user/sample_certs/ca.pem
  -------------------------------------------------------------
  9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08

  Expected end-state after successful completion
  ----------------------------------------------
  • Directory /home/user/db_ssl exists (permissions 755 or less restrictive).  
  • File /home/user/db_ssl/ca.pem exists and is identical (SHA-256 match) to
    /home/user/sample_certs/ca.pem.  
  • File /home/user/db_ssl/ca_checksum.log exists with *exactly*:

    Line 1: “CA_FILE=/home/user/db_ssl/ca.pem”
    Line 2: “SHA256=9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08”

    (No carriage returns, extra spaces, or additional lines.)

  The grader will fail the submission if:
  • Any path, filename, or digest in ca_checksum.log is incorrect.  
  • Additional text appears in ca_checksum.log.  
  • /home/user/db_ssl/ca.pem differs in any byte from the source file.
name: task_000000_83238c24
